focus: MVP v2 concision: one-product front-end unification, extraction resolution layers (memory vs whole transcript), attendance-based access model, styleguide-first design discipline, and six rulings amending the July 11 MVP-cut record
rationale: This document is a design-working-session record covering six product rulings on: (1) extraction architecture (whole-transcript extraction vs generated-memory views), (2) attendance-based access model, (3) styleguide-first component discipline, (4) amendments to the July 11 MVP-cut record (voice profiles/flocking viewer demoted, demo-share depth), (5) integration queue reordering (Teams), and (6) conversation-surface design language — all under the headline 'the live surface is the MVP product's front end that was never built.' I covered ArchitectureOverview's navigation/digest narratives (Narrative_DigestAsRecognitionAid, Narrative_NavigationReplacesEmbeddedSnapshot, Narrative_GraphInMemoryStorage) since the theatre-as-graph-viewer/resolution-layers idea directly extends 'navigation replaces embedded snapshot' and digest-as-recognition-aid. I pulled Narrative_MVPScopeDecision and its Refract/Registry-not-MVP positions since this is explicitly 'the second time she has made this exact move' (recurring strip-down discipline). Governance/Security areas (GovernanceSafetyTrust, SecurityPrivacyModel, Narrative_HubMemoryProvenanceNonNegotiable, Position_StaleGraphCitationsWorse, consent/RBAC positions, Vouch identity-for-agents, per-task OAuth scoping) were surfaced because the access-model ruling (attendance grants the page; digest narrations vs verbatim excerpts) bears directly on provenance-survives-summarization and stale-citation risk positions. PackagingPricing/PackagingEntitlement and RoleTopology/PrimaryActors were expanded because Teams-gates-Escher, tier access rules, and named-perspective/advisor sub-agent architecture (voice profiles, per-actor perspective filters) are explicitly referenced in the memo. CoreWorkflows and ModulesCapabilities ground the MCP tool surface (review, perspective) the theatre reply flow depends on. I deliberately did not expand deep style/rhetoric areas (Metaphor, Tagline, ToneVoice, etc.) or unrelated positioning narratives (SMB/B2C market pivots, Calm Company thesis) since this document is an internal product/design ruling record, not a positioning or messaging document, and those areas show no plausible overlap with its content.

## ^FrictionFailure  [44 children]
This hub tracks the friction points in AI adoption, development, and sales. Development friction is dominated by [AI code back-pressure ^Narrative_AIBackPressureCodeReview], where generation speed outpaces human review, and the [AI action trap ^Narrative_AIActionTrap], an infinite loop of ungrounded execution. These technical hurdles are compounded by [technical adoption friction ^Narrative_TechnicalAdoptionFriction], where users fear workflow contamination, and [identity onboarding gaps ^Narrative_IdentityOnboardingGap] that leave agents ungrounded. Sales and adoption are hindered by the [activation gap ^Narrative_ActivationGap], where the product is misperceived as an investment rather than an immediate tool, and [two-sided legibility problems ^Narrative_TwoSidedLegibilityProblem] that prevent practitioners from communicating their methodology. Organizational memory failures, such as [context hemorrhage ^Narrative_ContextHemorrhage] and [day-to-day re-litigation ^Narrative_DayToDayRelitigation], highlight the structural [forgetting bottleneck ^Narrative_ForgettingBottleneckIsRole] inherent in decision-junction roles. While [value is proven by showing ^Narrative_ShowVsDemoProof], the path to scale remains blocked by [internal organizational conflict ^Narrative_StaffingAIAdoptionWall] and the need for [ambient adoption ^Position_AmbientAdoption] beyond developer tooling.

### Narrative_HubCollectionMethodAtVolume — Hub-Collection Method at Volume: One Memory Per Hub, Not Per Transcript
type: Narrative
witnessed: 20260705

The method: analyze the whole set first — surface recurring topics, open questions, pivotal moments — then draft a small collection of memories, one per hub in the collection, each summarizing that theme with key quotes pulled from across the set. The user reviews the collection once, not every transcript. This is the self-serve, operator-driven form of the topic-analysis step from the three-part seeding methodology (May 1, the enterprise customer onboarding). Now written into the Onboarding Mode prompt for any user's agent to run.

### Position_ConvictionAndTemporalityAreDistinctAxes
type: Position
witnessed: 20260414 … 20260501
conviction: decision (origin, 2026-04-14)
source: Sample_PerspectiveTransforms_20260414
The extraction pipeline currently conflates conviction (how settled a claim is: notion/stake/boulder/foundation) with temporality (when a claim applies: now, soon, future, speculative). These are orthogonal axes. A claim can be high-conviction and speculative simultaneously — e.g., 'we should build X' stated with certainty during a brainstorm. Without separating these axes, speculative content gets encoded as current worldview. the customer's chief architect's demo pollution experience is the concrete production failure validating this design gap.
Conviction (how settled, built through agreement and action over time) and temporality (when something applies: now, soon, future, speculative) are orthogonal axes. The extraction pipeline currently conflates them. Actor transforms should surface temporality separately from conviction.

### Narrative_MVPScopeDecision — MVP Scope: Core vs. Future vs. Internal-Only

MVP is install-compile-cite-remember-review; Refraction, Registry, and marketplace features are post-MVP; internal tooling must be cleanly separated from the product
Core MVP (all tiers): Install expertise (connect a module/repo, possibly read-only), Compile (AI incorporates the module's knowledge), Cite/Provenance (verify where output comes from — current 'annotate' tool, needs rename), Remember (add own knowledge), Review (collaborative refinement — valuable at all tiers), Personal org-managed repo (individual+, no GitHub required), Account management, notifications. Not MVP — Publisher/Marketplace (future): Forking (requires GitHub), Refraction (quality tool for publishers), Content automation/stories, Marketplace distribution, GitHub account connection. Not MVP — Internal only (aswritten org tooling): Registry, Internal development tooling, Anything that serves the org-developing-the-product, not the product itself.
MVP Scope: Core vs. Future vs. Internal-Only

### Narrative_PerspectiveResponseOrderedByVolatility
type: Narrative
witnessed: 20260701
The perspective response is now structured in four fixed sections ordered by volatility: (1) the map — richest areas at steering grain, canonical order that never changes with the caller's focus; (2) the area index — every other area, named, one line each; (3) the active surface — recent and load-bearing witnesses, moves only when knowledge lands or recency window rolls; (4) the focus section, last — the only per-call material. ### Position_AbsenceIsNoRecognizedAssociation
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: decision (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
Absence is no recognized association, never nothing here. Targeted grounding may trust recognized-absence; creative exploration deliberately distrusts it and expands past the recognized. Creative reading costs expansion by design. A cheap legible-absence is dangerous precisely because the encounters it lets you skip are where novelty would have struck. Mitigation: honesty about scope (recognition aid, never account of meaning) and the always-reachable store.

witnessed: 20260616

source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
Expansion is steering, not just quoting. Expanding a subtree you genuinely lean on is the product working: you get the trajectory and variance, not just a verbatim quote. The bad case is expanding to discover whether anything is there; the good case is expanding onto material you have already decided is relevant. Route to the subtree, not the child.

### Position_IndexNeverGatesStore
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: principle (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
The index must never gate the store. Steering requires compression but compression via fixed themes is exactly the reductionism the discourse-act ontology exists to refuse. A claim is not about something; it is available to be read as many things. The digest does not have to anticipate the novel reading. It has to get you close enough via an old association that you expand and encounter the material.
